page.title: Scenarios:
page.howIntro: Build your eAuction scenario in 3 simple steps. Our engine analyses your inputs and recommends the best strategy.
page.step1Desc: Enter your total spend and number of suppliers to instantly check if an eAuction is the right approach for your negotiation.
page.step2Desc: Define your lots with quantities, supplier prices, and criteria. Each lot can have different preferences and competitive intensity levels, so our AI can tailor the recommendation per lot.
page.step3Desc: Get detailed insights per lot: recommended auction type, expected savings range, and configuration parameters — ready to launch your eAuction with confidence.
page.noResultsMatch: No results match your current filters
page.confirmBulkDelete: Are you sure you want to delete {count} scenario(s)?

v1.title: Negotiation Scenarios
v1.subtitle: Which auction format fits your context?

v2.title: Auction Selection Cascade
v2.intro: Start from the top. The first auction type whose conditions you meet is your best option.
v2.dsDesc: English + Dutch combined for maximum competition
v2.enDesc: Most options & transformations available (preference, ceiling, award…)
v2.duDesc: Price descends from known starting point
v2.jpDesc: Price ascends, sellers exit when too high
v2.sbDesc: Single blind submission, no live interaction

v3.title: Auction Compatibility Matrix
v3.intro: Match your situation against each column. The auction type with the most matches is your best fit.

v4.title: Quick Selector
v4.subtitle: Select criteria to find the best auction type
v4.emptyState: Select at least one criterion to see recommendations
v4.awardingHint: How do you want to decide?

v5.title: Crown Architect
v5.subtitle: Follow the questions to find the right auction type
v5.q1: Does the spend justify a real-time auction?
v5.q2: Do you have 3+ suppliers with close prices?
v5.q3: Do you want to award directly from the auction?
v5.dsShort: Combines English + Dutch for maximum competition
v5.dsDesc: The most powerful format. Runs an English reverse auction first, then a Dutch round to push prices further. Maximises savings through two-stage competitive pressure.
v5.dsUse: Ideal when you have many competitive suppliers and want to extract maximum value.
v5.enShort: Suppliers compete by lowering prices in real time
v5.enDesc: Suppliers bid against each other in real time, driving prices down. Supports overtime extensions, preference weighting, and ceiling prices. The most flexible format.
v5.enUse: Best when you have 3+ competitive suppliers and want full control over options.
v5.duShort: Price descends, first to accept wins
v5.duDesc: Price starts high and decreases over time. The first supplier to accept the current price wins the lot. Supports pre-bids for scheduled automatic bidding.
v5.duUse: Use when you want a binding award and have a known market price.
v5.jpShort: Price ascends, suppliers exit when too high
v5.jpDesc: Price starts low and increases round by round. Suppliers must confirm each round or exit. The last supplier standing wins. Great for price discovery.
v5.jpUse: Use when you don't need a binding award and want to discover the true market price.
v5.sbDesc: Each supplier submits one sealed offer without seeing other bids. Simple, fast, and requires minimal supplier engagement. No live interaction needed.
v5.sbUse: Use when the spend is small or suppliers won't participate in a live auction.
v5.trDesc: Traditional direct negotiation with suppliers. No auction platform involved. Use when competitive tension isn't possible or the category doesn't suit an auction format.
v5.trUse: Use when you have very few suppliers or the spend doesn't justify an electronic process.

hiw.overviewDesc: Crown Architect is an AI-powered tool that analyses your procurement context and recommends the best eAuction strategy. It evaluates 22 possible strategies across 6 dimensions to find the optimal match for each lot.
hiw.familiesIntro: Auction types are ranked from most efficient (highest savings potential) to most accessible. The engine evaluates which family best fits your context.
hiw.legendDesc: Short description of how this auction type works
hiw.legendOptions: Available configuration options for this type
hiw.legendClick: Click any card to see full details
hiw.scoringIntro: Each lot is scored across 6 dimensions. The engine combines your answers with a scoring matrix to compute a compatibility percentage for each of 22 strategies.
hiw.scoringMatch: The compatibility % shows how well a strategy fits your context compared to the best possible match.
hiw.scoringElimination: Strategies incompatible with your inputs are automatically eliminated (e.g. English auctions require ranking, Double Scenario needs 3+ suppliers).
hiw.conceptPreBidDesc: A scheduled bid placed before the auction starts. Executed automatically when the auction begins.
hiw.conceptPreferenceDesc: Weighting applied to favor certain suppliers. Can be non-financial (qualitative criteria) or financial (price-based weighting).
hiw.conceptAwardingDesc: How the winner is determined: Award (binding, best price wins), Rank (ordered but not binding), or No Rank (market testing only).
hiw.conceptIntensityDesc: The competitive pressure level: Collaborative (low pressure), Competitive (standard), or Aggressive (maximum pressure).
hiw.conceptEliminationDesc: Some auction types are incompatible with certain inputs. For example, 'No Rank' eliminates English and Double Scenario. Spend under 100K eliminates most live formats.
hiw.scoringTableIntro: Which options are available for each auction family:
hiw.exampleDs: e.g. 500 laptops, 5 suppliers, simple specs — maximum savings via English + Dutch rounds.
hiw.exampleEn: e.g. office furniture, 4 suppliers, binding award — real-time bidding with ceiling price.
hiw.exampleDu: e.g. fleet vehicles, 3 suppliers, known market price — descending price until first acceptance.
hiw.exampleJp: e.g. raw materials, 6 suppliers, price discovery — ascending rounds reveal true market price.
hiw.exampleSb: e.g. consulting services, 2 suppliers, no live auction — single sealed submission.
hiw.exampleTr: e.g. specialised equipment, 1 supplier — direct negotiation off-platform.
hiw.illustDs: Two-round strategy: English auction then Dutch round for maximum savings
hiw.illustEn: Suppliers compete in real-time by lowering their price until no one bids further
hiw.illustDu: Price starts high and drops automatically — first supplier to accept wins
hiw.illustJp: Price rises each round — suppliers exit when it's too high, last one standing wins
hiw.illustSb: Each supplier submits one sealed bid — no one sees others' prices
hiw.illustTr: Direct negotiation with the supplier — no auction mechanism
hiw.faq1Q: Can I change the auction type after starting?
hiw.faq1A: Yes — you can re-run the Architect at any time to get a new recommendation. Your previous configuration is saved.
hiw.faq2Q: What happens if no supplier bids?
hiw.faq2A: The auction closes without a winner. You can then adjust your strategy (lower intensity, different type) and re-launch.
hiw.faq3Q: How accurate are the savings estimates?
hiw.faq3A: The percentages are based on historical data across thousands of auctions. Actual savings depend on market conditions, supplier competition, and your negotiation leverage.
hiw.faq4Q: Do I need all suppliers registered before starting?
hiw.faq4A: No — you can invite suppliers after creating the auction. However, the recommendation accuracy improves when you know the exact number of participants.
hiw.lvlPreBidEnabledDesc: Suppliers submit a price before auction starts
hiw.lvlPrefNonFinDesc: Quality, CSR, delivery — weighted qualitative criteria
hiw.lvlPrefFinDesc: Price handicap/advantage applied to certain suppliers
hiw.lvlIntAggrDesc: Very high competitive pressure for maximum savings
hiw.optPreBidOnInfo: Suppliers submit a price before the auction opens. This pre-bid runs automatically at start, setting the initial competitive position.
hiw.optPreBidOffInfo: This auction type does not support pre-bids. All prices are submitted live during the auction.
hiw.optPrefAvailInfo: You can apply a weighting to favour certain suppliers (qualitative or financial adjustment).
hiw.optPrefNAInfo: This auction type does not support preference weighting.
hiw.optNoneInfo: No preference — all suppliers compete on equal footing.
hiw.optNonFinInfo: Non-financial — qualitative criteria (quality, CSR, delivery) weighted alongside price.
hiw.optFinInfo: Financial — a price adjustment (% or fixed amount) is applied to selected suppliers.
hiw.optAwardInfo: Binding — the winning supplier is contractually awarded the lot.
hiw.optRankInfo: Non-binding ranking — suppliers are ordered but no contract is signed.
hiw.optNoRankInfo: Market test only — prices collected with no ranking or commitment.
hiw.optAwardNAInfo: Traditional negotiation does not use an automated award mechanism.

lotTable.baselineTooltip: Average of active supplier unit prices, multiplied by quantity. This is the estimated total spend for each lot.
lotTable.intensityTooltip: Competition level for this lot (0–100%). Higher values indicate stronger competitive pressure, influencing which auction types are recommended.
lotTable.awardTooltipAward: Award — Best price wins the lot.
lotTable.awardTooltipRank: Rank — Suppliers are ranked, no binding commitment.
lotTable.awardTooltipNoRank: No Rank — No ranking, used for market testing.
lotTable.offersError: Please fill in all supplier offer fields before proceeding.

phase1.verdictWaitDesc: Input your event details to unlock negotiation recommendation
phase1.verdictStopDesc: Your current setup doesn't require an eAuction. A traditional negotiation is recommended.
phase1.verdictOkDesc: You can run an eAuction, but results may be limited with fewer suppliers or lower spend.
phase1.verdictPerfDesc: Your event is suited for an eAuction. Continue to the next step to configure your lot(s).
phase1.spendTooltip: Maximum spend consulted in the event
phase1.suppliersTooltip: Maximum number of suppliers consulted in the event
